warnings: USWDS Sass deprecation warnings (if-function, global-builtin) — pre-existing, not introduced by this change

dual_css_custom_properties.--pathable-font-mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace
dual_css_custom_properties.--usa-font-mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace

consistency_repairs.reason: Worker's initial implementation used $theme-font-role-* with direct typeface token names (fredoka, nunito, montserrat). This does not work in USWDS 3.13.0 because $theme-font-role-* values must be font type names (sans, serif, mono), not direct typeface token names. The role → font type → typeface token chain is: $theme-font-role-heading: 'serif' → $theme-font-type-serif: 'fredoka' → $theme-typeface-tokens['fredoka']. Core agent reverted to the correct USWDS indirection pattern and verified build (exit code 0) and dual CSS custom properties.

notes: font-family-heading and font-family-body USWDS utility classes not found in compiled CSS because $theme-global-content-styles is false (pre-existing setting). Brand fonts are correctly assigned via $theme-typeface-tokens → $theme-font-type-* → $theme-font-role-* chain.
$theme-font-role-* values use font type names (sans, serif, mono) per USWDS architecture — roles map to font types which map to typeface tokens in $theme-typeface-tokens.

Core agent repaired F001: reverted direct typeface token role assignments to USWDS indirection pattern (role → font type → typeface token). This is the correct USWDS 3.13.0 approach and compiles successfully.
